chunk_summary: Effective latch and frequent milk removal are key to breastfeeding success. Address tongue-tie if latch issues persist, and support mothers with behavioral strategies, workplace accommodations, and lactation guidance.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of latch optimization and frequent milk removal in early breastfeeding.
Recognize signs of tongue-tie and assess functional feeding impact.
Implement nonpharmacologic strategies to support milk supply and maternal well-being.
key_facts: Aim for 8–12 effective feeds per day in early infancy to stimulate milk production.
Routine top-ups without breast stimulation can worsen milk supply.
Tongue-tie assessment focuses on tongue elevation, protrusion, and functional feeding observation.
Frenotomy can improve maternal pain and milk transfer if tongue-tie is functionally significant.
Express milk at the same frequency as missed feeds when returning to work.
reference_quote: Assess for ankyloglossia when there is persistent maternal nipple pain, clicking, poor weight gain, or inability to maintain latch despite good positioning. (Source: NICE guidance IPG149)
document_key_topics: Nonpharmacologic strategies for milk supply support
